question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: addNftTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a single CRYPTOTRANSFER or TOKENTRANSFER transaction containing the protocol-maximum number of NFT transfer entries, causing addNftTransfer() to be called repeatedly and grow the nftTransfer ArrayList to its maximum size, resulting in a proportionally large JSONB payload written to the transaction table and sustained CPU/memory overhead during JSON serialization via ObjectToStringSerializer that increases mirror node processing resource consumption by over 30%?
